# ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0014 - QUIC RFC 9000 Connection-ID Retransmission Reliability Architecture

## Purpose

Describe the bounded send-runtime proof that packets carrying NEW_CONNECTION_ID and RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID payloads remain retransmittable when loss is registered and stay queued until the matching sent packet is acknowledged.

## Scope

This design covers only retransmission-ledger preservation for packets that already contain NEW_CONNECTION_ID or RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID payloads. It does not claim connection-ID issuance policy, peer CID registry updates, active connection-ID limit enforcement, retire-prior-to orchestration, migration routing, stateless-reset routing, packet builder scheduling, or interop migration success.

## Requirements Satisfied

- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P3-0029
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P3-0030

## Design Summary

The send-runtime retransmission ledger records sent application-data packets together with their packet bytes and retransmittable classification. When loss is registered for a tracked retransmittable packet, the runtime queues a retransmission plan that preserves the original packet number and bytes. ACK processing for unrelated packet numbers does not clear the queued repair, so NEW_CONNECTION_ID and RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID payloads remain repairable until the matching sent packet is acknowledged or dequeued for retransmission by the caller.

## Key Components

- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnectionSendRuntime.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P3-0029.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S13P3-0030.cs

## Data and State Considerations

This slice proves the sender-owned retransmission ledger behavior for already-built packet bytes. It does not create connection IDs, retire peer connection IDs, select active destination connection IDs, enforce active_connection_id_limit, or own migration routing state.

## Edge Cases and Constraints

- Loss registration must target the packet number that carried the control-frame payload.
- Unrelated ACKs must not clear queued connection-ID repair work.
- The retransmission plan must preserve the original packet bytes so the frame payload remains unchanged.
- The proof uses existing frame-codec builders to construct representative NEW_CONNECTION_ID and RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID payloads but does not widen codec behavior.

## Alternatives Considered

- Treat the broad RFC 9000 transport artifact as sufficient; rejected because the connection-ID repair requirements already have direct tests and benefit from focused proof links.
- Combine this with full connection-ID lifecycle management; rejected because issuing, retiring, active-limit policy, migration routing, and public API behavior are separate state-machine slices.

## Risks

- Overstating this proof as full connection-ID lifecycle support would hide remaining state-machine work.
- Clearing queued repair on unrelated ACKs would create loss-dependent control-frame disappearance.
- Rebuilding instead of preserving packet bytes could silently change the retransmitted frame payload.
